text: Which among the following polymers exhibits hydrogen bonding?
explanation:
**Nylon-6,6** is a **polyamide** that shows strong **intermolecular hydrogen bonding** between the N–H and C=O groups of adjacent polymer chains:

$$\cdots\text{–N–H}\cdots\text{O=C–}\cdots$$

This H-bonding is responsible for Nylon-6,6's high strength and use as a fibre.

The other polymers:
- **Polyvinyl chloride** (–CH₂–CHCl–)ₙ: No N–H or O–H groups → no H-bonding
- **Neoprene** (polychloroprene): No H-bonding groups
- **Teflon** (–CF₂–CF₂–)ₙ: Fully fluorinated, no H-bonding capability
name: Polymers Item 1

text: Intermolecular forces in Nylon-6,6 are:
explanation:
**Nylon-6,6** is a **polyamide** — its polymer chains contain **–CO–NH–** (amide) linkages.

The primary intermolecular forces are **hydrogen bonds** formed between:
- The **N–H** group of one chain (H-bond donor)
- The **C=O** group of an adjacent chain (H-bond acceptor)

This extensive H-bonding gives Nylon-6,6 its characteristic properties:
- High tensile strength
- High melting point
- Ability to be drawn into strong fibres

This is why it is classified as a **fibre** (not an elastomer or thermoplastic).
name: Polymers Item 2

text: Which of the following is a biodegradable polymer of the polyamide class?
explanation:
**Nylon-2-nylon-6** is a **biodegradable polyamide**.

Classification of the given polymers:

| Polymer | Class | Biodegradable? |
|---|---|---|
| Dextran | Polysaccharide | Yes |
| Nylon-2-nylon-6 | **Polyamide** | **Yes** ✓ |
| Nylon-6,6 | Polyamide | No |
| PHBV | Polyester | Yes |

Nylon-2-nylon-6 is an alternating copolymer of glycine (H₂N–CH₂–COOH, 2 carbons) and ε-aminocaproic acid (6 carbons). It is biodegradable because the amide bonds can be hydrolysed by microorganisms.
name: Polymers Item 3

text: Which of the following is an example of a chain-growth polymer?
explanation:
**Chain-growth polymerisation** (addition polymerisation) involves unsaturated monomers adding one at a time to a growing chain with an active site (free radical, cation, or anion). No small molecules are eliminated.

- **Teflon** (polytetrafluoroethylene) is made by chain-growth polymerisation of tetrafluoroethylene (CF₂=CF₂): $$n\text{CF}_2\text{=CF}_2 \rightarrow (-\text{CF}_2\text{-CF}_2-)_n$$

Other options:
- **Bakelite:** Condensation (step-growth) polymer ✗
- **Nylon:** Condensation (step-growth) polymer ✗
- **Terylene (Dacron):** Condensation (step-growth) polymer ✗
name: Polymers Item 4

text: Which among the following polymers can be formed by using caprolactam as the monomer unit?
explanation:
**Nylon-6** is produced from **caprolactam** (a cyclic amide/lactam with 6 carbons).

The polymerisation occurs by ring-opening of caprolactam when heated at 533–543 K in water:

$$\text{caprolactam} \xrightarrow{533\ \text{K}, \text{H}_2\text{O}} \left(-\text{CO}-(\text{CH}_2)_5-\text{NH}-\right)_n$$

This gives Nylon-6 (polycaprolactam), which is also known as **Perlon**.

Note: **Nylon-6,6** is made from hexamethylene diamine + adipic acid (two different monomers, not caprolactam).
name: Polymers Item 5

text: Which of the following polymers is biodegradable?
explanation:
**Nylon-2-nylon-6** is the only biodegradable polymer among the given options.

| Polymer | Biodegradable? |
|---|---|
| Nylon-6,6 | No — resistant to microbial degradation |
| **Nylon-2-nylon-6** | **Yes** ✓ |
| Melamine polymer | No — highly cross-linked, very stable |
| Nylon-6 | No |

Biodegradable polymers that are important to know: **PHBV** (polyester), **Nylon-2-nylon-6** (polyamide), and **Dextran** (polysaccharide).
name: Polymers Item 6

text: Which among the following is an example of a fibre?
explanation:
**Polymers are classified as:**
- **Fibres:** High tensile strength, crystalline, high melting point (e.g., polyamides, polyesters)
- **Plastics:** Rigid or semi-rigid materials (e.g., PVC, polythene)
- **Elastomers:** Rubber-like, can be stretched and return to original shape (e.g., neoprene)
- **Thermosetting resins:** Cross-linked, cannot be remelted (e.g., Bakelite, urea-formaldehyde)

**Polyester** (e.g., Dacron/Terylene) is a **fibre** — used in textile manufacturing due to its high strength and inability to be easily stretched.

- Urea-formaldehyde resin → thermosetting resin ✗
- PVC → plastic ✗
- Neoprene → elastomer ✗
name: Polymers Item 7

text: Me₃SiCl is used during polymerisation of organosilicones because:
options: The chain length of organosilicone polymers can be controlled by adding Me₃SiCl
Me₃SiCl improves the quality of the silicone polymer
Me₃SiCl improves the yield of the silicone polymer
Me₃SiCl acts as a catalyst during polymerisation
explanation:
**(CH₃)₃SiCl** (trimethylsilyl chloride) is used as a **chain terminator** in the polymerisation of organosilicones.

When added to the polymerising silicone mixture, it **blocks the ends** of the growing silicone chain:
$$\cdots\text{-Si(R)}_2\text{-OH} + \text{(CH}_3)_3\text{SiCl} \rightarrow \cdots\text{-Si(R)}_2\text{-O-Si(CH}_3)_3 + \text{HCl}$$

By **controlling the amount of (CH₃)₃SiCl added**, chemists can control the **chain length** (and thus the molecular weight and viscosity) of the final silicone polymer. More terminator = shorter chains.
name: Polymers Item 8

text: The polymer Glyptal is formed by which of the following monomer combinations?
explanation:
**Glyptal** (polyethylene glycol phthalate) is a **polyester** formed by the condensation polymerisation of:
- **Ethylene glycol** (HO–CH₂–CH₂–OH)
- **Phthalic acid** (ortho-C₆H₄(COOH)₂)

$$n\text{HO-CH}_2\text{-CH}_2\text{-OH} + n\text{(COOH)}_2\text{-C}_6\text{H}_4 \rightarrow [\text{-O-CH}_2\text{-CH}_2\text{-OOC-C}_6\text{H}_4\text{-CO-}]_n + 2n\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

Glyptal is a thermosetting resin used in paints and lacquers. It is different from Dacron/Terylene (which uses **terephthalic acid**, not phthalic acid).
name: Polymers Item 9

text:
Match the following:

| List I | List II |
|---|---|
| A. Teflon | I. SnCl₂ |
| B. Anionic polymerisation | II. CF₂=CF₂ |
| C. Cationic polymerisation | III. Bakelite |
| D. Thermosetting polymer | IV. Polystyrene |
| | V. RLi |
explanation:
- **A. Teflon → II (CF₂=CF₂):** Teflon (PTFE) is made by addition polymerisation of tetrafluoroethylene monomer ✓

- **B. Anionic polymerisation → V (RLi):** Organolithium compounds (R–Li) are used as initiators for anionic polymerisation because they generate carbanions ✓

- **C. Cationic polymerisation → I (SnCl₂):** Lewis acids like SnCl₂, BF₃, AlCl₃ initiate cationic polymerisation by generating carbocations ✓

- **D. Thermosetting polymer → III (Bakelite):** Bakelite is a classic thermosetting polymer — it cross-links irreversibly on heating and cannot be remelted ✓

Correct match: **A-II, B-V, C-I, D-III**
name: Polymers Item 10

text:
How many of the following polymers come under the category of condensation polymers?

Bakelite, Teflon, Nylon-6, Dacron, Polyisoprene, Melamine polymer, Neoprene
explanation:
Classification:

**Condensation polymers** (form with elimination of small molecules like H₂O):
1. **Bakelite** → phenol + formaldehyde (condensation) ✓
2. **Nylon-6** → caprolactam ring-opening (condensation) ✓
3. **Dacron (Terylene)** → ethylene glycol + terephthalic acid (condensation) ✓
4. **Melamine polymer** → melamine + formaldehyde (condensation) ✓

**Addition polymers** (no elimination):
5. **Teflon** → CF₂=CF₂ (addition) ✗
6. **Polyisoprene** → isoprene (addition) ✗
7. **Neoprene** → chloroprene (addition) ✗

**Total condensation polymers = 4**
name: Polymers Item 11

text:
Match the following:

| List I | List II |
|---|---|
| A. Addition polymer | I. Bakelite |
| B. Condensation polymer | II. 2-methyl-1,3-butadiene |
| C. Acrilan | III. 2,3-dimethyl-1,3-butadiene |
| D. Rubber | IV. Vinyl cyanide |
| | V. Polythene |
explanation:
- **A. Addition polymer → V (Polythene):** Polythene is made by addition polymerisation of ethylene (CH₂=CH₂). No small molecules eliminated. ✓

- **B. Condensation polymer → I (Bakelite):** Bakelite is made by condensation of phenol and formaldehyde with elimination of water. ✓

- **C. Acrilan → IV (Vinyl cyanide/Acrylonitrile):** Acrilan (polyacrylonitrile) is made from vinyl cyanide (CH₂=CH–CN, acrylonitrile). ✓

- **D. Rubber → II (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene/Isoprene):** Natural rubber is *cis*-polyisoprene, made from isoprene (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene). ✓

Correct match: **A-V, B-I, C-IV, D-II**
name: Polymers Item 12

text: The monomers of Nylon-6,6 (X) and Terylene (Y) are respectively:
options: X: H₂N–(CH₂)₆–NH₂ and HOOC–(CH₂)₄–COOH; Y: HO–CH₂CH₂–OH and HOOC–C₆H₄–COOH (terephthalic acid)
X: H₂N–(CH₂)₆–NH₂ and HOOC–C₆H₄–COOH; Y: HO–CH₂CH₂–OH and HOOC–(CH₂)₄–COOH
X: caprolactam only; Y: HO–CH₂CH₂–OH and HOOC–C₆H₄–COOH
X: H₂N–(CH₂)₂–COOH and H₂N–(CH₂)₅–COOH; Y: HO–CH₂CH₂–OH and HOOC–C₆H₄–COOH
explanation:
**Nylon-6,6 (X)** is made by condensation of two monomers, each with 6 carbons:
- **Hexamethylenediamine:** H₂N–(CH₂)₆–NH₂ (6 carbons)
- **Adipic acid:** HOOC–(CH₂)₄–COOH (6 carbons including both carboxyl carbons)

$$n\text{H}_2\text{N-(CH}_2)_6\text{-NH}_2 + n\text{HOOC-(CH}_2)_4\text{-COOH} \rightarrow [\text{-CO-(CH}_2)_4\text{-CO-NH-(CH}_2)_6\text{-NH-}]_n + 2n\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

**Terylene/Dacron (Y)** monomers:
- **Ethylene glycol (ethan-1,2-diol):** HO–CH₂–CH₂–OH
- **Terephthalic acid (para-benzenedicarboxylic acid)**

These condense to form the polyester Dacron.
name: Polymers Item 13

text: The catalyst triethylaluminium and titanium tetrachloride is used in the formation of which polymer?
explanation:
**Triethylaluminium (C₂H₅)₃Al + Titanium tetrachloride TiCl₄** together form the **Ziegler-Natta catalyst**.

This catalyst is used for the preparation of **High Density Polyethylene (HDPE)**:

$$n\text{CH}_2\text{=CH}_2 \xrightarrow{\text{TiCl}_4/(\text{C}_2\text{H}_5)_3\text{Al}} (-\text{CH}_2\text{-CH}_2-)_n$$

**HDPE properties:**
- Linear chains (no branching)
- Higher density than LDPE
- Made at lower pressure and temperature than LDPE
- Stronger and more rigid than LDPE

LDPE is made at high pressure (~2000 atm) using peroxide initiators — no Ziegler-Natta catalyst.
name: Polymers Item 14

text: Number average molecular mass of a polymer that contains 15 molecules with each of mass 8,000 and 15 molecules with each of mass 80,000 is:
explanation:
**Number average molecular mass** is given by:
$$\bar{M}_n = \dfrac{\sum N_i M_i}{\sum N_i}$$

Given:
- 15 molecules of mass 8,000
- 15 molecules of mass 80,000

$$\bar{M}_n = \dfrac{(15 \times 8000) + (15 \times 80000)}{15 + 15}$$

$$= \dfrac{120000 + 1200000}{30} = \dfrac{1320000}{30} = 44000$$

**Number average molecular mass = 44,000**

Note: This equals the **arithmetic mean** of the two mass values: $$\dfrac{8000 + 80000}{2} = 44000$$ (because there are equal numbers of each type).
name: Polymers Item 15

text: In anionic polymerisation, the compound which acts as an effective chain initiator is:
explanation:
**Anionic polymerisation** is initiated by nucleophiles/bases that generate **carbanions**.

**R-Li (alkyllithium compounds)** are effective initiators for anionic polymerisation:
$$\text{R-Li} \rightarrow \text{R}^- + \text{Li}^+$$
The carbanion (R⁻) attacks the monomer's π-bond, generating a new carbanion at the end of the chain.

**Comparison of initiators:**
- **BF₃:** Lewis acid → cationic polymerisation ✗
- **(CH₃CO)₂O₂ (acetyl peroxide):** Free radical initiator ✗
- **SnCl₂:** Lewis acid → cationic polymerisation ✗
- **R-Li:** Generates carbanion → **anionic polymerisation** ✓
name: Polymers Item 16

text: Catalyst used in the manufacture of high density polythene is:
explanation:
**High Density Polyethylene (HDPE)** is manufactured using the **Ziegler-Natta catalyst**: $$\text{TiCl}_4 + (\text{C}_2\text{H}_5)_3\text{Al}$$

This combination was developed by Karl Ziegler and Giulio Natta (Nobel Prize 1963).

**Why Ziegler-Natta gives HDPE:**
- Catalytic polymerisation at low temperature and pressure
- Produces **linear, unbranched** polymer chains
- Linear chains pack closely → **high density** and high crystallinity
- Results in stronger, more rigid polymer than LDPE

In contrast, LDPE is made at high pressure (~200 MPa) using peroxide initiators, giving branched chains with lower density.
name: Polymers Item 17

text: Which one of the following structures represents neoprene rubber?
explanation:
**Neoprene** (polychloroprene) is a synthetic rubber made by free radical polymerisation of **chloroprene** (2-chloro-1,3-butadiene, CH₂=CCl–CH=CH₂):

$$n\text{CH}_2\text{=CCl-CH=CH}_2 \rightarrow [-\text{CH}_2\text{-CCl=CH-CH}_2-]_n$$

The repeating unit has:
- A **chlorine atom** on one of the double bond carbons
- Alternating single and double bonds in the backbone

**Structure (a):** $$[-\text{CH}_2\text{-C(Cl)=CH-CH}_2-]_n$$ correctly represents neoprene ✓

Neoprene is resistant to oils, heat, and oxidation — used in wetsuits, hoses, and gaskets.
name: Polymers Item 18

text: Nylon-6,6 is a condensation polymer of two monomers X and Y. The number of –CH₂– groups in X and Y are respectively:
explanation:
**Nylon-6,6** is synthesised by polycondensation of:

- **Hexamethylenediamine (X):** H₂N–(CH₂)₆–NH₂
  - Contains **6 –CH₂– groups** ✓

- **Adipic acid (Y):** HOOC–(CH₂)₄–COOH
  - Contains **4 –CH₂– groups** (the 2 carbon atoms of the –COOH groups are not –CH₂– groups) ✓

The name "Nylon-6,6" comes from the fact that **both monomers contribute 6 carbon atoms each** to the polymer chain (hexamethylenediamine has 6 C, adipic acid has 6 C including the carboxyl carbons).

**CH₂ groups: X = 6, Y = 4**
name: Polymers Item 19

text: Examples of a synthetic polymer (X) and a semi-synthetic polymer (Y) are:
explanation:
**Synthetic polymers** are completely man-made (synthesised from petrochemicals/simple molecules with no natural polymer starting material).

**Semi-synthetic polymers** are made by chemically modifying a **natural polymer**.

- **Polythene (X):** 100% synthetic — made by polymerisation of ethylene from petroleum ✓

- **Rayon (Y):** Semi-synthetic — made by chemically treating **natural cellulose** (wood pulp) with NaOH and CS₂ (viscose process). It is regenerated cellulose. ✓

Other semi-synthetic polymers: Cellulose acetate, cellulose nitrate, vulcanised rubber.

Nylon-6,6 and PVC are fully synthetic polymers.
name: Polymers Item 20
